description_long: This clock tower features a rich, dark wooden structure with a polished finish, accented by gold metallic trim and decorative finials at the top corners. The front face is dominated by a large, circular clock with a white background, Roman numerals, and two black hands, all encased in a gold bezel. The body of the tower is rectangular with a gently arched roof, topped by a gold ring handle. The sides and back are solid wood panels, with the back mirroring the arched shape of the roof and lacking any clock face or openings. The base is broad and sturdy, with gold feet and trim, giving the object a sense of permanence and elegance. The overall design is reminiscent of neoclassical mantel clocks, blending traditional craftsmanship with ornamental details.
description: A neoclassical wooden clock tower with gold trim, a prominent Roman numeral clock face, and an arched roof. The structure is elegant, with polished wood and decorative gold accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large white clock face with Roman numerals, gold bezel, and decorative gold finials.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid wooden panel, gold trim, and the arched roof profile without any clock face.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the arched wooden back, gold handle, and absence of any clock face or openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the solid wood panel and gold trim.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular with a white background, featuring black Roman numerals for the hours and simple black tick marks for the minutes. It has two black hands (hour and minute), and the face is encircled by a gold bezel. The design is classic and uncluttered, with no additional decorative elements on the face itself.
